emails.body:
James,

Heard through the grapevine that a rotation board is meeting next month. Any word on whether you're looking at a move or staying put? I'm starting to think about my own options and would value your perspective.

Thanks,
Jeff

Jeff,

Good to hear from you. The rotation board is still firming up dates, but I haven't seen anything concrete for my billet yet. On the project side, ATaS is tracking toward a sub‑1 % error rate and we're prepping a Senate briefing for later this quarter. That's keeping the team busy.

Let me know if you want to compare notes on the board process.

Best,
James

LCDR Martinez,

We received your copy of the earlier thread and wanted to confirm whether you have any pending orders to San Diego. The detailer's office is finalizing the next cycle and we need to verify your preference.

Please advise at your earliest convenience.

V/R,
JIAITF Security Office

James,

Thanks for the update. I'm weighing a few career paths—shore duty, a staff tour, or possibly a joint assignment. Your insight on the board timeline would help.

Also, I had a chance to review the v13 briefing deck you sent over. Slide 14 appears to carry a classification banner that doesn't belong in an unclassified package. Could you double‑check that slide and coordinate with the security office on a possible spillage report?

Appreciate the heads‑up on the board and the deck.

Respectfully,
Jeff

Jeff,

Appreciate the catch on the deck—I'll pull slide 14 right away and run it by security. I haven't had a chance to look at v18 yet, but the 1 % figure still looks defensible based on v17 validation.

On the rotation front, I'm still waiting on the final board schedule. I'll keep you posted once I have firm dates.

Thanks again,
James
